NIST-Aligned Review Focus: User accounts should be approved reviewed modified and disabled based on documented procedures
GRC Review Notes: Access management should include evidence of account approvals periodic reviews and timely removal of unnecessary access.
Remediation Action: Create a recurring user access review process with assigned owners review dates approval evidence and documented follow-up actions.

NIST-Aligned Review Focus: Users should only have access required for their job responsibilities
GRC Review Notes: Privileged accounts require stronger review because elevated access can increase security exposure if not monitored.
Remediation Action: Document privileged account reviews require manager approval for elevated access and retain review evidence in a centralized location.

NIST-Aligned Review Focus: Security incidents should be documented escalated tracked and resolved using an approved process
GRC Review Notes: Incident response should include severity levels escalation paths roles responsibilities and closure documentation.
Remediation Action: Create or update the incident response playbook with severity definitions escalation steps ownership and post-incident review requirements.

NIST-Aligned Review Focus: Audit logs should be reviewed for suspicious unauthorized or unusual activity
GRC Review Notes: Collecting logs is not enough for audit readiness. The organization should show evidence that logs are reviewed on a defined schedule.
Remediation Action: Define log review frequency assign a control owner document review results and retain evidence of monitoring activity.

NIST-Aligned Review Focus: Users should complete cybersecurity awareness training on a defined schedule
GRC Review Notes: Training supports cybersecurity culture and helps reduce user-driven risk such as phishing weak passwords and mishandling sensitive information.
Remediation Action: Continue annual training tracking maintain completion reports and review participation metrics with leadership.

NIST-Aligned Review Focus: Security policies should be documented approved reviewed and updated as needed
GRC Review Notes: Policies should include document owners review dates approval history and version control so they can support audit readiness.
Remediation Action: Add policy owners review cadence approval history version control and last-reviewed dates to governance documents.

NIST-Aligned Review Focus: Vulnerabilities should be identified prioritized assigned tracked and remediated
GRC Review Notes: Vulnerability management should show how findings are prioritized by severity assigned to owners and tracked through closure.
Remediation Action: Create a vulnerability remediation tracker with severity ratings due dates responsible owners status updates and closure evidence.

NIST-Aligned Review Focus: Organizations should maintain review and test continuity plans
GRC Review Notes: Continuity plans should be supported by testing evidence lessons learned and corrective actions.
Remediation Action: Schedule annual continuity testing document test results capture lessons learned and track corrective actions to completion.
